description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and prominent flanges at the base and midsection. The hydrant features two large side outlets and a smaller front-facing outlet, all capped, with visible wear and chipping in the red paint revealing a metallic underlayer. The top of the hydrant has a hexagonal operating nut, used for opening the valve, and the body is adorned with raised ridges and bolts for structural reinforcement. The surface shows signs of weathering, with patches of rust and grime, typical of hydrants exposed to outdoor urban environments. No chains are attached to the caps, and there are no visible manufacturer markings or logos. The overall design is robust, intended to withstand high pressure and frequent use in emergency situations.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three capped outlets, a domed top, and visible signs of rust and paint chipping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main cylindrical body, domed top, and two large side outlets with caps; paint is chipped and worn.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the smaller front-facing outlet and the hexagonal operating nut on top; the base flanges are clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with both large side outlets visible and the back of the cylindrical body exposed.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the smaller rear-facing outlet and the domed top; surface wear and rust are evident.
